behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for a loom item. It provides an examine ('U8P_LOOM::look') handler that makes the item emit/display its name and a use handler that animates/toggles the loom's visual frame while playing a sound and spawning short-lived helper tasks between steps.
LOOM::look causes the loom item to 'bark' the literal string "loom " to the requesting process/player so the item presents its name/description when inspected.

LOOM::use plays a sound effect, reads the current frame, and then either steps frames upward from 0 to 7 or downward from the current frame to 0. After each frame change it spawns a helper (FREE::1C19) and suspends, producing a paced animation sequence.
notable_control_flow: LOOM::look: On Event 0 (U8P_LOOM::look), the routine sets process info/exclude bits, pushes the pid and item pointer, calls Item::bark with the fixed string "loom ", handles the call return, then suspends/returns.

LOOM::use: On use it first plays a sound (playSFX), then gets the item's frame (Item::getFrame). If the frame is zero it initializes an ascending loop from 0 to 7; otherwise it initializes a descending loop from current frame down to 0. Each loop iteration sets the frame (Item::setFrame), spawns FREE::1C19 with (pid, 0x05, 0), and suspends between iterations until the sequence completes.
open_questions: What precise visual or gameplay effect FREE::1C19 implements (appears to be a short delay/animation step helper but not directly observed).
What the third parameter passed to playSFX controls (inferred volume/priority but not named concretely here).
Whether any additional state or external systems observe the frame changes beyond the item instance itself (no globals are read/written in this excerpt).

main_function.name: U8P_LOOM::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the player/actor inspecting the loom by causing the loom item to emit/display its name/description string.
entry_conditions: Event 0: player or actor looks at / inspects the loom (enterFastArea-style U8P_LOOM::look interaction)

function_map.name: U8P_LOOM::look
function_map.role: Examine/inspect handler: invoke Item::bark to present "loom " to the viewer.

function_map.name: U8P_LOOM::use
function_map.role: Use/activate handler: play a sound and animate the loom by stepping frames up or down, spawning short-lived helper tasks between steps.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to emit/display a short text message from an item/process to a player/process.

function_map.name: U8P_LOOM::playSFX
function_map.role: Play a sound effect when the item is used (called at start of LOOM::use).

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Return the item's current animation/frame index (used to decide animation direction).

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Set the item's animation/frame index during the animation sequence.

function_map.name: spawn FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawn a short-lived helper/task after each frame change (appears to implement a per-step delay/visual helper).

external_calls.purpose: Make the loom present the string "loom " to the viewing process/player (examine feedback).
Play a sound effect when the loom is used to provide auditory feedback at animation start.
Query the loom's current frame to decide whether to animate upward from 0->7 or downward to 0.
Apply each sequential frame value to the loom during the animation/toggle sequence.
Spawn a helper process/task after each frame change; likely provides a short delay or visual/animation helper between steps.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for a loom item. It provides an examine ('U8P_LOOM::look') handler that makes the item emit/display its name and a use handler that animates/toggles the loom's visual frame while playing a sound and spawning short-lived helper tasks between steps.
